hero.question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing a 1-in-4 shot at U.S. military action against Iran before 2027, down sharply from 39.5% yesterday, suggesting either genuine de-escalation or panic selling into bad overnight news. The real tell is the volume surge to $6.2M, which usually means informed traders are loading up on NO, and they're probably right given that actual invasion requires congressional appetite nobody has shown.

daily_take.headline: Iran Invasion Odds Crater 33 Points. Smart Money Sees De-Escalation, Not War.
daily_take.deck: The market just repriced a US-Iran military conflict down hard, signaling either genuine diplomatic progress or a short-term relief rally. The real tell: simultaneous bets on a US-Iran meeting by April 2026 spiked 64 points to 77%, suggesting traders believe containment over confrontation. At 26.5%, invasion odds look oversold if regional tensions remain elevated, but if talking actually happens, you want to short this further.

sidebar.headline: The diplomatic hedge is real. Meetings soar while invasion tanks, meaning markets now price in negotiation as the base case, not escalation.
sidebar.label: US x Iran Meeting by April 2026: 77.0%

sidebar.headline: Monte Carlo tennis moves 31 points on Zverev action. Check if this is genuine form shift or noise before piling in.
sidebar.label: Garin vs Zverev: 43.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic normalization jumped 24 points in tandem with invasion odds falling. One market confirms the other. If you believe in de-escalation, this is a tighter spread play than direct Iran conflict exposure.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Normal by End April: 36.5%
